hero.question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
hero.prob_take:
At 25.5%, the crowd is pricing in a 1-in-4 shot that shipping flows through Hormuz normalize by April 30, which feels like they're underweighting how quickly geopolitical tensions can deflate when attention spans shift elsewhere.

The real tell is the 7-point jump in 24 hours, signaling fresh shorts covering or news flow about diplomatic backchannels, so before chasing this move higher watch for actual tanker traffic data and whether US carrier positioning suggests preparation for de-escalation.

daily_take.headline: Hormuz Traffic Spike Signals Market Missed the Memo on Iran
daily_take.deck: Odds on Strait of Hormuz normalization by April jumped 7 points to 25.5% overnight on $9M volume, a massive move for a niche geopolitical bet. The market is pricing in either a major de-escalation or capitulation from whoever's been disrupting shipping. Smart money is asking: does fresh intel suggest talks are actually moving, or is this just noise from a thin book? Check shipping data and Iranian rhetoric before chasing the move.

sidebar.headline: López Aliaga's -21 point crater in 24 hours screams something broke in Peru. Watch for polling updates or coalition drama before the move reverses.
sidebar.label: Rafael López Aliaga 2026: 5.5%

sidebar.headline: Weibo Gaming got smoked down 24 points, but liquidity is thin at $315K. Could be sharp action or could be one big bet. Check recent form before fading.
sidebar.label: Weibo Gaming vs iG BO3: 21.5%

sidebar.headline: Nuclear deal odds cratered to 19.5% on zero volume. This market is dead. Don't touch it until real money shows up.
sidebar.label: US-Iran Nuclear Deal 2025: 19.5%

b.thesis: Crowds overprice long shots. Sell the overpriced tickets.

c.name: The Arb
c.strategy: Trade Poly vs Kalshi gaps: bet the lagging platform when gap ≥10pts
c.thesis: When two liquid markets disagree by 10+ points, one is wrong.
